# Hadoop (HDFS) Connector - Full Documentation

# Hadoop (HDFS) Connector - Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Create a Text File in HDFS

The following example shows how to use the connector to create a text file in HDFS:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, click **File > New > Mule Project**, name the project, and click **Finish**.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** search field, type `http`.
3.  Drag the **HTTP > Listener** operation to the canvas.
4.  In **General** settings, click the green plus sign (+) to the right of **Connector
    Configuration**.
5.  Click **OK** to accept the default settings.
6.  In **Path**, enter `/createFile`.
7.  In the **Mule Palette** search field, type `hdfs`.
8.  Drag the HDFS **Write** operation to the canvas.
9.  Set **Path** to `/test.txt` and leave the default values for the other options.  
    This is the path of the file that HDFS creates.
10. Right-click in the project canvas and select **Run project** to start the application.
11. From an HTTP client such as Postman or curl, make a POST request, set the value for
    `Content-type:plain/text` to `localhost:8081/createFile`, and add the content you want to write
    as a payload, as shown in the following example:  
    `curl -X POST -H "Content-Type:plain/text" -d "payload to write to file" localhost:8090/createFile`
12. Use an HDFS explorer to verify that the `/test.txt` file exists and contains your content.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# HDFS (Hadoop) Connector Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Hadoop (HDFS) v6.0

Release Notes:
[Hadoop (HDFS) Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/hdfs-connector-release-notes-mule-4)

## HDFS Configurations

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#hdfs_kerberos">Kerberos</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#hdfs_simple">Simple</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Connection Types

To access the data in a Hadoop (HDFS) instance, you must authenticate your applicationâs requests
using a supported authentication method.

For Hadoop (HDFS) Connector, the following connection authentication types are supported:

- [Kerberos](#hdfs_kerberos)
- [Simple](#hdfs_simple)

### Kerberos

Use the following fields in the Global Element Properties dialog window to configure the
**Kerberos** connection type:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Kerberos principal. The Username is passed to the HDFS client as the <code>hadoop.job.ugi</code> configuration entry. The Username can be overridden by values you specify in <strong>Configuration Resources</strong> and <strong>Configuration Entries</strong>. This parameter is called Username for backward compatibility reasons.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keytab Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the path to the <a href="https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-1.12/doc/basic/keytab_def.html">keytab file</a> associated with the Username you specified. The path is used to obtain a ticket granting ticket (TGT) from the authorization server. If this value is not provided Kerberos looks for a TGT associated with the specified Username within your local Kerberos cache.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name Node Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the file system to connect to. The Name Node is passed to the HDFS client as the <code>FileSystem#FS_DEFAULT_NAME_KEY</code> configuration entry. The Name Node can be overridden by values you specify in <strong>Configuration Resources</strong> and <strong>Configuration Entries</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration Resources</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A <code>java.util.List</code> of configuration resource files for the HDFS client to load. You can provide additional configuration files, for example, <code>core-site.xml</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A <code>java.util.Map</code> of configuration entries to use by the HDFS client. You can provide additional configuration entries as key-value pairs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If reconnection is enabled, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Simple

Use the following fields in the Global Element Properties dialog window to configure the **Simple**
connection type:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>User identity that Hadoop uses for permissions in HDFS. When Simple authentication is used, Hadoop requires that the user is set as a system property called <code>HADOOP_USER_NAME</code>. If a value is not provided, Hadoop uses the username of the OS user who is currently logged in.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name Node Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the file system to which to connect. The Name Node is passed to the HDFS client as the <code>FileSystem#FS_DEFAULT_NAME_KEY</code> configuration entry. The Name Node can be overridden by values you specify in <strong>Configuration Resources</strong> and <strong>Configuration Entries</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration Resources</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A <code>java.util.List</code> of configuration resource files for the HDFS client to load. You can provide additional configuration files, for example, <code>core-site.xml</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A <code>java.util.Map</code> of configuration entries for the HDFS client to use. You can provide additional configuration entries as key-value pairs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If reconnection is enabled, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [Append](#append)
- [Copy From Local File](#copyFromLocalFile)
- [Copy To Local File](#copyToLocalFile)
- [Delete Directory](#deleteDirectory)
- [Delete File](#deleteFile)
- [Get Metadata](#getMetadata)
- [Glob Status](#globStatus)
- [List Status](#listStatus)
- [Make Directories](#makeDirectories)
- [Read Operation](#readOperation)
- [Rename](#rename)
- [Set Owner](#setOwner)
- [Set Permission](#setPermission)
- [Write](#write)

### Associated Sources

- [Read](#read)

### Append

`<hdfs:append>`

Append the current payload to a file located at the designated path. **Note:** by default the Hadoop
server has the append option disabled. To append data to an existing file, refer to the
dfs.support.append configuration parameter.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file to write to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The buffer size to use when appending to the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>4096</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Payload</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The payload to append to the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Copy From Local File

`<hdfs:copy-from-local-file>`

Copy the source file on the local disk to the file system for a target path, set **Delete Source**
if the source file should be removed.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delete Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to delete the source.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to overwrite destination content.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The source path in the file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The target path on the local disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Copy To Local File

`<hdfs:copy-to-local-file>`

Copy the source file in the file system to a local disk at the given target path. Set **Delete
Source** if the source file should be removed. **Use Raw Local File System** indicates whether to
use RawLocalFileSystem as it is a non-CRC file system.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delete Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to delete the source.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Raw Local File System</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use RawLocalFileSystem as a local file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The source path on the File System.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The target path on the local disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Delete Directory

`<hdfs:delete-directory>`

Delete the file or directory located at the designated path.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Delete File

`<hdfs:delete-file>`

Delete the file or directory located at the designated path.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Get Metadata

`<hdfs:get-metadata>`

Get the metadata of a path

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#MetaData">Metadata</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Glob Status

`<hdfs:glob-status>`

Return all the files that match file pattern and are not checksum files. Results are sorted by their
names.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path Pattern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A regular expression specifying the path pattern.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user supplied path filter</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#FileStatus">File Status</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### List Status

`<hdfs:list-status>`

List the statuses of the files and directories in the given path if the path is a directory.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The given path</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user supplied path filter</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#FileStatus">File Status</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Make Directories

`<hdfs:make-directories>`

Make the given file and all non-existent parents into directories. Has the semantics of Unix 'mkdir
-p'. Existence of the directory hierarchy is not an error.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to create one or more directories.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Permission</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The file system permission to use when creating the directories, either in octal or symbolic format (umask).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Read Operation

`<hdfs:read-operation>`

Read the content of a file designated by its path and streams it to the rest of the flow.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file to read.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The buffer size to use when reading the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>4096</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Rename

`<hdfs:rename>`

Renames path target to path destination.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The source path to be renamed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>New path after rename.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Set Owner

`<hdfs:set-owner>`

Set owner of a path for a file or a directory. The Ownername and Groupname cannot both be null.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file or directory to set owner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ownername</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If it is null, the original username remains unchanged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Groupname</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If it is null, the original groupname remains unchanged.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Set Permission

`<hdfs:set-permission>`

Set permission of a path, that is, for a file or a directory.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file or directory to set permission.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Permission</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The file system permission to be set.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

### Write

`<hdfs:write>`

Write the current payload to the designated path, either creating a new file or appending to an
existing one.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Payload</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>the payload to write to the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file to write to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Permission</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The file system permission to use if a new file is created, either in octal or symbolic format (umask).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>700</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If a pre-existing file should be overwritten with the new content.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The buffer size to use when appending to the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>4096</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replication</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Block replication for the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Block Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The block size to use when appending to the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1048576</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Owner User Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The username owner of the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Owner Group Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The group owner of the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

#### Throws

- HDFS:CONNECTIVITY
- HDFS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- HDFS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- HDFS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- HDFS:UNKNOWN

## Sources

### Read

`<hdfs:read>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read the content of a file designated by its path</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>4096</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether this source should be executed only on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

[HDFS Configurations](#hdfs)

## Types

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to reconnect (in milliseconds)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of reconnection attempts to make</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of memory that will be allocated to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used, the STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more memory is consumed, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering a process-failed message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is SHA-256.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property can only be set if <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Metadata

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Check Summary</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#CheckSummary">Check Summary</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Summary</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ContentSummary">Content Summary</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Status</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#FileStatus">File Status</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path Exists</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Check Summary

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Bytes Per CRC</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Crc Per Block</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Md5</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Content Summary

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Directory Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Directory Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot File Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Snapshot Space Consumed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### File Status

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Access Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Access time of file in milliseconds</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Block Replication</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Replication factor of file</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Block Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Block size of file</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Directory</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether path is a directory</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Group</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Group owner associated with file</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Length</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Length of file in bytes</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Modification Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Modification time of file in milliseconds</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Owner</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Owner of file</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path name</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Permission</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Permission of file as an octal string</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Symbolic Link</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether a path is a symbolic link</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Hadoop (HDFS) Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  Create a Mule project.
2.  Add the connector to your Mule project
3.  Configure an input source for the connectorâs flow.
4.  Configure global elements for the connector.

If you are new to configuring Anypoint Connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).

## Create a Mule Project

In Anypoint Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector.

1.  In Anypoint Studio, from the main menu, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project, and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Hadoop (HDFS) Connector to a Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location, as well as add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file.

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `hdfs` in the search field.
3.  Click the name of the connector in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that same connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure an Input Source

An input source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. The act of dragging the
connectorâs operation from the palette onto the Anypoint Studio canvas automatically populates the
XML code with the connector namespace and schema location.

Possible input source operations for this connector are:

- Read  
  Starts your app when the content of a file is read and streamed to the rest of the flow.
- HTTP Listener  
  Starts your app using a browser or a command such as `curl`.
- Scheduler  
  Starts your app at timed intervals.

In Studio, drag the input operation you choose from the **Mule Palette** to the **Source** panel in
the canvas, and supply the required parameters.

For example, the required fields for the `Read` input source are:

- Configuration  
  The name of the connector configuration to use
- Path  
  The path for the file to read

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

To configure a connector in your Mule app, configure a global element that can be used by all
instances of that connector in the flow:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for the connector and select an operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the canvas to the right of the input source.
3.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to add a global element for the connector.
4.  In the **General** tab, enter a name for the configuration, and select the connection type from
    **Connection**.  
    Supported connection types for this connector are:
    - [**Kerberos**](hdfs-connector-reference#hdfs_kerberos)
    - [**Simple**](hdfs-connector-reference#hdfs_simple)

      For information about how to configure the connections, see
      [Connection Types](hdfs-connector-reference#connection_types).

      You can provide your credentials in the global configuration properties, or you can reference
      a configuration file that contains those values.

      To simplify maintenance and improve project reusability, of your project, use a configuration
      file. Keeping these values in a separate file is useful if you need to deploy to different
      environments, such as production, development, and QA, where your access credentials differ.

      See
      [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview)
      for more information.

5.  Click the **Advanced** tab to specify a reconnection strategy, if supported.
6.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
7.  Click **OK**.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Hadoop (HDFS) Connector Reference](hdfs-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Hadoop (HDFS) Connector - XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you manually code a Mule app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML editor or from a text
editor, configure access to the connector in your app by adding reference namespace statements to
your XML Mule flow and a dependency in the Apache Maven POM file.

> [!NOTE] You can avoid this manual process by using Anypoint Studio. When you add the connector in
> Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the connectorâs namespace and schema
> location, and adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Reference Namespace Statement for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/hdfs
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/hdfs/current/mule-hdfs.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:hdfs="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/hdfs"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/hdfs
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/hdfs/current/mule-hdfs.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains Mule app connector
dependencies. When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your POM file to enable
access to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
	<groupId>org.mule.connectors</groupId>
    <artifactId>mule-hdfs-connector</artifactId>
    <version>6.0.3</version>
    <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Verify that the connector `version` corresponds to the one you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `hdfs`.
4.  Click **Dependency Snippets**.

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Hadoop (HDFS) Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Hadoop (HDFS) Connector Version 6.0

Anypoint Connector for Hadoop (HDFS) works as a bidirectional gateway between Mule applications and
Apache Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS). You can use the Hadoop (HDFS) Connector to poll the
content of a file at a configurable rate or interval, or to manipulate data in an HDFS server.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, see
[Hadoop (HDFS) Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/hdfs-connector-release-notes-mule-4)

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements in a Mule flow
- Global elements
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Access to the Hadoop (HDFS) target resource and Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio version 7.0 or later
- A running instance of Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS)

## Connection Authentication Types

Hadoop (HDFS) Connector supports the following connection authentication types:

- Kerberos  
  This connection type uses the Kerberos principal as the username. The keytab path you specify
  obtains a ticket-granting ticket (TGT) from the authorization server.
- Simple  
  This connection type passes the name of the node that contains the file system you want to connect
  to as the default name key.

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites and experiment with templates and examples, you are ready to
create an app with [Anypoint Studio](hdfs-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Hadoop (HDFS) Connector on Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/?search=hdfs&type=connector)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
